# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.
# Category: api_gateway_security

_format_version: "3.0"

# Global Plugins
# These plugins are applied to all services and routes unless specifically overridden.
plugins:
  # Prometheus plugin for exposing Kong's metrics
  - name: prometheus
    config: {} # Basic configuration, metrics usually exposed on /metrics
  # CORS plugin to handle cross-origin requests
  - name: cors
    config:
      origins:
        - "http://localhost:3000"
        - "https://www.your-frontend.com"
        - "https://your-mobile-app-domain.com"
      methods:
        - "GET"
        - "POST"
        - "PUT"
        - "PATCH"
        - "DELETE"
        - "OPTIONS"
      headers:
        - "Content-Type"
        - "Authorization"
        - "X-Requested-With"
      exposed_headers:
        - "X-Rate-Limit-Limit"
        - "X-Rate-Limit-Remaining"
        - "ETag"
      credentials: true
      max_age: 3600 # 1 hour

# Upstreams: Define the load balancing groups for your microservices
upstreams:
  - name: auth-service-upstream
    slots: 100 # Maximum number of health check slots for targets
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /health # Health check endpoint
        timeout: 1 # Timeout for health check requests
        interval: 5 # Interval between active health checks
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 3 # Mark target unhealthy after 3 HTTP failures
          timeouts: 2 # Mark target unhealthy after 2 timeouts
          # http_statuses: [500, 502, 503, 504] # Custom unhealthy statuses
        healthy:
          successes: 3 # Mark target healthy after 3 successful checks
          http_statuses: [200, 201] # Custom healthy statuses
      passive:
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 5 # Mark target unhealthy after 5 passive HTTP failures
          timeouts: 3
          # http_statuses: [500, 502, 503, 504]
        healthy:
          successes: 3

  - name: user-service-upstream
    slots: 100
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /status # Example health endpoint
        timeout: 2
        interval: 10
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 3
          timeouts: 2
        healthy:
          successes: 2
      passive:
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 5
          timeouts: 3

  - name: product-service-upstream
    slots: 100
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /actuator/health # Common Spring Boot Actuator endpoint
        timeout: 1
        interval: 5
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 2
          timeouts: 2
        healthy:
          successes: 3
      passive:
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 5
          timeouts: 3

  - name: admin-service-upstream
    slots: 100
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /healthz
        timeout: 1
        interval: 10
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 3
          timeouts: 2
        healthy:
          successes: 3
      passive:
        unhealthy:
          http_failures: 5
          timeouts: 3

# Targets: Specific instances of your microservices within upstreams
targets:
  - upstream: auth-service-upstream
    target: auth-app-prod-01:8080 # Internal DNS name or IP:Port
    weight: 100
  - upstream: auth-service-upstream
    target: auth-app-prod-02:8080
    weight: 100
  - upstream: user-service-upstream
    target: user-app-prod-a:3000
    weight: 100
  - upstream: user-service-upstream
    target: user-app-prod-b:3000
    weight: 100
  - upstream: product-service-upstream
    target: products-api-v1:4000
    weight: 100
  - upstream: product-service-upstream
    target: products-api-v2:4000 # Example for potential different version/instance
    weight: 100
  - upstream: admin-service-upstream
    target: admin-portal-internal:5000
    weight: 100

# Services: Define your backend services that Kong will proxy to
services:
  - name: auth-service
    host: auth-service-upstream # Referencing an upstream
    port: 80 # Default port to connect to targets if not specified in target
    protocol: http
    retries: 5 # Number of retries on upstream failure
    connect_timeout: 60000 # ms
    write_timeout: 60000 # ms
    read_timeout: 60000 # ms
    routes:
      - name: auth-route
        paths:
          - /auth # Matches requests to api.example.com/auth
        strip_path: true # Remove /auth from the request path before forwarding
        protocols:
          - http
          - https
        hosts:
          - api.example.com
          - auth.api.example.com # Can define multiple hosts
        methods:
          - POST
          - GET # For login/token refresh and potentially /auth/status

  - name: user-service
    host: user-service-upstream
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 3
    plugins: # Service-level plugins for user-service
      - name: jwt # Enforce JWT authentication
        config:
          claims_to_verify:
            - exp # Verify token expiration
          uri_param_names: # Allow JWT in URI param for specific cases (e.g., websocket)
            - jwt_token
      - name: rate-limiting # Apply rate limits
        config:
          minute: 100 # Max 100 requests per minute per consumer
          hour: 1000 # Max 1000 requests per hour per consumer
          policy: local # Use Kong's local memory for rate limiting (can be 'redis')
          fault_tolerant: true # Allow requests if rate-limiting backend is down
          hide_client_headers: false # Show X-Rate-Limit headers to client
      - name: acl # Access control based on consumer groups
        config:
          allow:
            - authenticated_users # Allow consumers in 'authenticated_users' group
            - admin # Allow consumers in 'admin' group
    routes:
      - name: user-profile-route
        paths:
          - /users
          - /users/(.*) # Matches /users and sub-paths like /users/profile
        strip_path: true
        protocols:
          - http
          - https
        hosts:
          - api.example.com
        methods:
          - GET
          - POST
          - PATCH
          - DELETE

  - name: product-service
    host: product-service-upstream
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 3
    plugins: # Service-level plugins for product-service
      - name: jwt
        config:
          claims_to_verify:
            - exp
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 200 # Higher limit for product browsing
          hour: 2000
          policy: local
    routes:
      - name: product-catalog-route
        paths:
          - /products
          - /products/(.*)
        strip_path: true
        protocols:
          - http
          - https
        hosts:
          - api.example.com
        methods:
          - GET
          - POST
          - PUT
          - DELETE
          - PATCH

  - name: admin-service
    host: admin-service-upstream
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 3
    plugins: # Service-level plugins for admin-service
      - name: jwt
        config:
          claims_to_verify:
            - exp
      - name: acl
        config:
          allow:
            - admin # Only allow consumers in 'admin' group
    routes:
      - name: admin-dashboard-route
        paths:
          - /admin
          - /admin/(.*)
        strip_path: true
        protocols:
          - http
          - https
        hosts:
          - api.example.com
        methods:
          - GET
          - POST
          - PUT
          - DELETE
          - PATCH

# Consumers: Define users or applications that consume your APIs
consumers:
  - username: internal-admin-app
    custom_id: internal-app-001
    tags:
      - internal
      - admin
    plugins:
      - name: jwt # JWT credential for this consumer
        config:
          algorithm: HS256 # Algorithm used to sign the JWT
          key: admin_app_key # This key should match 'iss' claim in JWT or 'kid' header
          secret: your_super_secret_jwt_for_admin_app_key # The shared secret for signing/verifying
      - name: acl # Assign this consumer to the 'admin' group
        config:
          group: admin

  - username: web-client-app
    custom_id: web-client-002
    tags:
      - external
      - web
    plugins:
      - name: jwt
        config:
          algorithm: HS256
          key: web_client_key
          secret: your_super_secret_jwt_for_web_client_key
      - name: acl # Assign this consumer to the 'authenticated_users' group
        config:
          group: authenticated_users

  - username: mobile-client-app
    custom_id: mobile-client-003
    tags:
      - external
      - mobile
    plugins:
      - name: jwt
        config:
          algorithm: HS256
          key: mobile_client_key
          secret: your_super_secret_jwt_for_mobile_client_key
      - name: acl
        config:
          group: authenticated_users

  - username: public-service-account
    custom_id: public-service-004
    tags:
      - public
    plugins:
      # No JWT here if this account isn't meant to use JWT for auth,
      # or specify a different credential type (e.g., key-auth)
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key: public_api_key_12345
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 10 # More restrictive rate limit for public access
          hour: 100
          policy: local

# Certificates (if you're terminating SSL/TLS at Kong)
# certificates:
#   - id: my-cert-id-123
#     cert: |
#       -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
#       ... your certificate ...
#       -----END CERTIFICATE-----
#     key: |
#       -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
#       ... your private key ...
#       -----END PRIVATE KEY-----
#     snis: # SNIs (Server Name Indication) associated with this certificate
#       - api.example.com
#       - auth.api.example.com
